input_candidates.truth_value: The lath martensite start temperature calculated by empirical equation is ~350°C, supporting martensite formation upon water quenching.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Product of ultimate tensile strength and total elongation (PSE): HRQ 1.01 GPa%, IA750_5 Min 2.5 GPa%, IA750_30 Min 4.3 GPa%, IA750_60 Min 7.9 GPa%, IA750_120 Min 2.8 GPa%, IA750_180 Min 3.1 GPa%.

input_candidates.truth_value: In dilatometry, only a slight deviation at ~600°C was observed, attributed to possible dissolution of carbide/cementite or ferrite; no significant transformation of ferrite to austenite.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Volume fraction of austenite and mechanical stability parameter (K) before/after tensile test: HRQ ~19% to ~18% (K~3.26, transformation ratio ~5.5%); IA750_5 Min ~46% to ~42% (K~1.93, ratio 8.7%); IA750_30 Min ~40% (K 1.10); IA750_60 Min ~35% (K~1.92, ratio ~20%); IA750_120 Min and IA750_180 Min ~30%.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fractography: HRQ specimen shows cleavage/flat facets (brittle failure); annealed specimens show quasi-cleavage with some dimples from reverted austenite; overall brittle due to high δ-ferrite content.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Work hardening rate (WHR) behavior of annealed specimens shows two stages (S1, S2). Mean WHR in stage S2: IA750_5 Min 1100-400 MPa, IA750_30 Min 1700-1000 MPa, IA750_60 Min 2000-1000 MPa, IA750_180 Min 1500-900 MPa.

input_candidates.truth_value: No TWIP effect observed despite high Al; serrations absent in stress-strain curves; deformation mechanism mainly TRIP.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fractography of HRQ specimen exhibits mainly cleavage and flat facets indicating brittle failure. Annealed specimens (IA750_5 Min to IA750_180 Min) show quasi-cleavage fracture; delta ferrite contributes to cleavage while reverted austenite produces dimples. Majority of fracture surface remains brittle due to high delta ferrite fraction.

input_candidates.truth_value: SFE calculated for equilibrium austenite composition at 750°C: Al 4.39%, C 0.47%, Mn 11.16%, Si 1.09% leading to ~35 mJ/m2; but actual revereted austenite composition changes SFE to ~27 mJ/m2.
input_candidates.prediction_value: No TWIP effect observed in the developed steel despite high theoretical SFE (~35 mJ/m²). Dominant deformation mechanism is TRIP. Actual SFE of IA750_60 Min reverted austenite recalculated as ~27 mJ/m², and potentially ~2 mJ/m² at lower C content (~0.05 wt.%).

input_candidates.truth_value: Austenite mechanical stability K calculated by Sugimoto's model: HRQ K~3.26, IA750_5Min K~1.93, IA750_30Min K~1.10, IA750_60Min K~1.92, IA750_120Min and 180Min K values higher (lower stability).
input_candidates.prediction_value: Dilatometry curve of hot-forged sample shows insignificant contraction during heating/cooling, confirming stable delta ferrite remains during solidification and minimal austenite decomposition to martensite after hot forging. Slight deviation at ~600°C corresponds to carbide/cementite dissolution.

input_candidates.truth_value: The product of strength and elongation (PSE) is highest for IA750_60Min at 7.9 GPa%.
input_candidates.prediction_value: APT quantitative concentration profile for IA750_60 Min: reverted austenite contains Fe 72.92±0.33 at.%, Mn 12.41±0.24 at.%, Al 11.74±0.32 at.%, Si 1.34±0.08 at.%, C 1.59±0.09 at.%; intercritical ferrite contains Fe 77.94±0.35 at.%, Mn 5.65±0.36 at.%, Al 14.41±0.14 at.%, Si 1.76±0.12 at.%, C 0.24±0.12 at.%. Interface composition: ~65% Fe, ~18% Mn, ~11% Al, ~1.7% Si, 2.40% C.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fig. 8 provides a schematic representation of martensite dissolution and austenite reversion during annealing.
input_candidates.prediction_value: The hot rolled sheet thickness before surface machining was approximately 5 mm (inferred from removal of 1 mm per side yielding final 3 mm sheet).
